title: rc.3 deferred fixes: multiline-safe quoteIfNeeded + orphan-on-partial-failure hardening + (conditional) date filter for search
description: Address 3 of the 5 rc.3 deferred items per planning context. (a) Multiline-safe quoteIfNeeded: services/review.ts:923-929 helper currently does NOT escape \n in title/summary values, producing malformed YAML frontmatter when a modify call passes multiline strings — patch to escape \n + \r → emit single-line scalar. (b) Orphan-on-partial-failure hardening: services/review.ts approveOne (L339-393) has a window between canonical write and pending unlink — patch to scan target canonical dir for files starting with `<slug>--` BEFORE allocate; if found (resumption case from prior failure), re-attach existing canonical (no new id allocation) and emit knowledge_promoted with reason='[resumed]' prefix; mitigates slug-prefix collision. (c) Date filter for search (CONDITIONAL): if api-contracts.ts FabReviewSearchInput permits adding optional `created_after` field non-breaking → IN SCOPE; else DEFER and document in test plan.

files.target: quoteIfNeeded helper at L923-929 + approveOne at L339-393 + (conditional) listPending/searchPending at L173-178
files.change: (a) quoteIfNeeded: add newline check at top — if value.includes('\n') || value.includes('\r'), replace with literal backslash-n / backslash-r in output AND ensure surrounding quotes (return JSON.stringify(value) for single-line preservation, or hand-rolled `"${value.replaceAll('\n', '\\n').replaceAll('\r', '\\r')}"` if JSON.stringify side effects undesired). (b) approveOne pre-allocate guard: before calling allocator.allocate(layer, type), scan dest dir <projectRoot>/.fabric/knowledge/<layer>/<type>/ for files matching pattern `^K[TP]-${typeCode}-\d+--${slug}\.md$`; if exactly 1 match: parse stable_id from existing filename, treat as resumption — emit knowledge_promote_started + knowledge_promoted with reason='[resumed]:slug-prefix-collision' + return that stable_id (skip allocate, skip git mv since canonical already exists; just unlink pending if still present); if 0 match: proceed with allocate as today; if >1 match: throw (ambiguous — operator triage). (c) created_after filter: read api-contracts.ts FabReviewSearchInput schema; if `created_after?: z.string().optional()` can be added without breaking discriminated union → patch schema + extend listPending filter logic with `if (filters.created_after && entry.created_at < filters.created_after) skip`; if schema requires tighter discriminated-union surgery → log decision to TASK-006 commit msg and DEFER.

files.target: extend with 3 new sub-describes: 'quoteIfNeeded multiline', 'approveOne resumption', '(conditional) search created_after'

files.change: If schema permits (verify by reading current shape and running zod parse with extra field): add optional `created_after: z.string().datetime().optional()` to search action's discriminated branch. If extension breaks: DO NOT modify; document in commit msg + planning-context update.

implementation: Read services/review.ts L923-929 (quoteIfNeeded) to confirm current logic; identify minimal diff for newline escape

Patch quoteIfNeeded: add early check at top: `if (typeof v === 'string' && /[\n\r]/.test(v)) return JSON.stringify(v);` — JSON.stringify escapes \n → \\n and quotes the value, producing valid single-line YAML scalar; preserves backward compat for non-multiline values (existing logic unchanged)
Read services/review.ts L339-393 (approveOne) to lock the current sequence (parse → allocate → git mv → write id → emit events)

Implement pre-allocate slug-prefix guard: BEFORE allocator.allocate, compute destDir = `<projectRoot>/.fabric/knowledge/<layer>/<type>/`; readdirSync(destDir) (catch ENOENT → 0 match), filter by regex `/^(K[TP]-${typeCode}-\d+)--<slug>\.md$/` (escape slug); if exactly 1 match → parse stable_id from filename, set isResumption=true, prior_stable_id=parsedId, skip allocate, skip git mv (canonical exists); after this branch, if isResumption: still emit knowledge_promote_started + knowledge_promoted with reason field set to `[resumed]:slug-prefix-collision`; on 0 match → existing allocate-and-mv flow; on >1 match → throw 'ambiguous canonical matches for slug — operator triage required'
If pending file still exists after resumption check: fs.unlink it (or git rm if under git) — completes the partial-failure recovery; emit a debug log

Read packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts FabReviewSearchInput shape; verify if it's a discriminated union branch; identify whether `created_after?: z.string().datetime().optional()` is non-breaking (additive optional field on existing discriminated branch is non-breaking in zod)
If non-breaking: add `created_after: z.string().datetime().optional()` to FabReviewSearchInput discriminated branch in api-contracts.ts; update FabReviewSearchInput exported type

If non-breaking: extend services/review.ts listPending/searchPending filter logic — for each entry, if filters.created_after && entry.created_at < filters.created_after: skip; ensure filter applies to both pending and canonical search modes
If breaking (rare for additive optional): DEFER created_after; commit msg explicitly notes 'created_after deferred to v2.1 — schema unlock required'
Add 6-8 unit tests in review.test.ts new sub-describes
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server typecheck && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test`; verify all green
Commit as 'fix(review): rc.3 deferred items — multiline-safe quoteIfNeeded + slug-prefix resumption + (date filter if applicable) (rc.4 TASK-006)'

reference.pattern: rc.3 review.ts approveOne; quoteIfNeeded helper; FabReviewSearchInput discriminated union
reference.examples: rc.3 final code review section 'Critical 1 path-traversal' (defense-in-depth pattern); rc.3 'Medium 2 multiline quoteIfNeeded' (deferred item rationale)

criteria: quoteIfNeeded handles strings with \n: returns single-line escaped form; round-trip YAML.parse on output preserves the original string (verified by round-trip test)
quoteIfNeeded handles strings with \r: returns escaped form (verified)
quoteIfNeeded backward compat: non-multiline strings unchanged in output (verified by snapshot of existing behavior)

approveOne with pre-existing canonical at slug-prefix match resumes: returns matching stable_id, emits knowledge_promoted with reason starting '[resumed]:', no new id allocated (verified by allocator counter unchanged before/after)
approveOne with 0 canonical match: existing allocate-and-mv flow (verified — regression test on rc.3 happy path)
approveOne with >1 canonical match: throws clear error 'ambiguous canonical matches' (verified)
If date filter IN SCOPE: search action with created_after filter excludes entries older than threshold (verified)
If date filter IN SCOPE: search action without created_after filter returns all entries as before (regression test)
If date filter DEFER: commit msg + planning-context.md updated to note deferral
review.ts coverage maintains >= 90% lines after patches (rc.3 baseline 93.97%)
0 type errors; 0 regression in existing 30+ review.test.ts tests

rationale.chosen_approach: Surgical 3-fix patch on review.ts; conditional date-filter based on schema unlock cost; preserves rc.3 architecture while addressing deferred items the rc.3 review.md explicitly listed as cheap fixes for rc.4
alternatives_considered: Add created_after to discriminated union as a separate action variant — rejected: adds complexity for minimal value; optional field is cleaner if non-breaking
Skip multiline-safe fix (low likelihood real users hit it) — rejected: rc.3 review explicitly recommended this as cheap; takes 5 LOC
Use simple-git library for slug-prefix scan — rejected: existing fs.readdirSync precedent
Add knowledge_promote_resumed event type — rejected: schema unlock; reason-prefix '[resumed]:' is rc.3 convention precedent
decision_factors: All 3 items explicitly cheap per rc.3 review.md disposition
No schema breaking changes (date filter conditional on additive-optional verification)
Slug-prefix recovery aligns with rc.3 filesystem-edit fallback philosophy
Multiline fix mitigates real malformed-YAML risk if upstream contract relaxes
rationale.tradeoffs: Date filter may DEFER if schema unlock required — accepted; planning context already documents conditional disposition; slug-prefix scan adds O(M files in dest dir) work to approveOne hot path (mitigated: typical dirs have <100 files)

manual_checks: Manually craft a modify call with title containing \n; verify resulting frontmatter is single-line valid YAML
Manually delete a pending file mid-approve (simulate partial failure), retry approve with same slug → confirm resumption with [resumed] reason
If date filter shipped: search with created_after threshold returns expected subset

risks.description: Slug-prefix scan regex incorrectly matches unrelated files (e.g., slug='foo' matches 'foobar')
risks.mitigation: Anchor regex with `--` separator after slug: `/^(K[TP]-${typeCode}-\d+)--${escapeRegex(slug)}\.md$/` (no greedy match); add test for slug='foo' vs file 'KT-D-0001--foobar.md' → 0 match
risks.fallback: If regex still over-matches in dogfood, switch to startsWith + endsWith check

risks.description: Date filter schema extension may break existing FabReviewSearchInput consumers in unforeseen ways
risks.mitigation: Verify additive-optional non-breaking by running existing review.test.ts after schema patch; if any test breaks, revert + DEFER

risks.description: JSON.stringify-based quoteIfNeeded may double-quote already-quoted strings
risks.mitigation: Existing quoteIfNeeded already checks for need-to-quote conditions; multiline check happens BEFORE other checks; round-trip test catches double-quote
risks.fallback: Hand-rolled escape if JSON.stringify proves too coarse

key_functions.purpose: Single-line YAML scalar emit; multiline-safe via JSON.stringify on \n/\r presence

key_functions.signature: async function findExistingCanonicalBySlugPrefix(destDir: string, typeCode: string, slug: string): Promise<{stableId: string, filename: string} | null>
key_functions.purpose: Pre-allocate guard scanning dest dir for slug-prefix matches
